content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Barbara Coler 
Fairfax Town Councilmember 
14 Ace Court 
Fairfax, CA 94930 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-14-076 
Dear Ms. Coler: 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
May 2, 2014 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions 
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because the Fair Political Practices Commission (the 
"Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders assistance (111 re Oglesby ( 1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), this letter is based on the facts presented. We also note that we do not provide 
advice on past conduct and our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. (Section 
83114(b); Regulation 18329(a).) 
QUESTION 
Do you have a disqualifying conflict of interest that prohibits you from participating in 
decisions related to the property located at 2626 Sir Frances Drake Blvd. in Fairfax? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. It is reasonably foreseeable that the decisions will have a material financial effect 
on your real property interest, however the small jurisdiction public generally exception applies, 
allowing you to participate. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Tille 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references arc to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-14-076 
Page No. 2 
You are a member of the Fairfax Town Council. Fairfax covers 2.2 square miles in 
Marin and has a population of just under 7,500. Your term on the town council extends four 
years from November of 2013, when you were elected in an at-large vote. 
Fairfax is in the process of rezoning several parcels to Planned Development District 
("PDD") to be consistent with the General Plan that the Council adopted in April of 2012. The 
POD rezoning only impacts three or four properties, one of which is over 300 feet and under 500 
feet from your townhouse, which you have owned and lived in since 1996. This property is 
located at 2626 Sir Frances Drake Boulevard in Fairfax. The PDD rezone of this property would 
allow the construction of senior low cost housing on part of the property (but the school and 
church would remain as well). Other properties that are the subject of the rezoning decisions are 
not near your townhouse. 
On the advice of counsel, you have thus far recused yourself from all rezoning decisions 
in which the property at 2626 Sir Frances Drake is involved. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or 
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a 
financial interest. A public official has a "financial interest" in a decision, within the meaning of 
the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on 
one or more of the public official's interests. (Section 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) Typically, 
we apply a standard analysis to determine whether an individual has a disqualifying conflict of 
interest. You have already moved through the beginning of the analysis with your Town's legal 
counsel, so we focus only on the pertinent steps. 
You have already identified that you are a public official for purposes of the Act by virtue 
of your position on the Town Council. (See Section 87200.) You have also stated that the Town 
Council will be making decisions in which you will participate. (Regulation 18702.2.) You own 
your townhouse in Fairfax, and as such have a real property interest. (Regulation 18703.2.) 
Is it reasonably foreseeable that the decisions will have a material financial effect on your 
financial interests? 
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 
87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, 
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her 
immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic interests. (Section 87103; 
Regulations 18703-18703.5.) Your property is within 500 feet of one of the subject properties. 


File No. A-14-076 
Page No. 3 
The Act presumes a material financial effect with such proximity.2 Given the proximity and that 
the planned project of building a senior living center at the site, it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision (which will lead to that project), will have a material effect on your property. 
Consequently, you have a disqualifying conflict of interest. 
Does an exception to the conflict-of-interest rules apply? 
Public Generally Exception 
Even if an official has a conflict of interest, disqualification is not required if the 
governmental decision affects the public official's interests in a manner that is indistinguishable 
from the manner in which the decision will affect the public generally. (Section 87103; 
Regulation 18707(a).) Particularly pertinent to your account of the facts is Regulation 18707.10, 
which establishes the public generally exception for an official's domicile in a small jurisdiction. 
Under Regulation 18707.10 and barring a reasonably foreseeable financial effect on any other 
interests the official may have, an official with an interest in a residential real property that is the 
official's domicile may make, participate in making, or influence a governmental decision with a 
financial effect on the real property under the following conditions: 
"(I) The jurisdiction of the public official's agency has a population of 30,000 
or less and covers a geographic area of ten square miles or less; 
"(2) The public official is required to live within the jurisdiction; 
"(3) The public official, if elected, has been elected in an at-large jurisdiction; 
"( 4) The official's property is more than 300 feet from the boundaries of the 
property that is the subject of the governmental decision; 
"(5) The official's property is located on a lot not more than one-quarter acre 
in size or not larger than 125 percent of the median residential lot size for the 
jurisdiction; and 
"(6) There are at least 20 other properties under separate ownership within a 
500 foot radius of the boundaries of the property that is the subject of the 
governmental decision that are similar in value." (Regulation 18707.10.) 
Based on your facts, the public generally exception for a public official's domicile in a 
small jurisdiction applies to your property interest. You may therefore make, participate in 
2 A financial effect need nol be likely 10 be considered reasonably foreseeable. In general, if the financial 
effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or lheorelical, ii is reasonably 
foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary circumstances nol subject 10 the public 
official's control, ii is nol reasonably foreseeable. (See also, Regulation 18705.2(a)(l 1).) 


File No. A-14-076 
Page No. 4 
making, or influence decisions related to the property at 2626 Sir Frances Drake, which is within 
500 feet of your property, as well as the other properties that are the subject of the ordinance. 3 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HMR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Heather M. Rowan 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
3 The Act recognizes that there may be instances where a governmental body cannot function without the 
participation or a public official who has a conflict of interest. The Act contains a narrow exception to the conflict­
of-interest rules codified at Section 8710 I allowing an official, otherwise disqualified from a governmental decision, 
to participate in the decision if the official's participation is "legally required." You have not offered facts to 
support the legally required exception.

decisions related to the property located at 2626 Sir Frances Drake Blvd. in Fairfax?

CONCLUSION: Yes. It is reasonably foreseeable that the decisions will have a material financial effect 
on your real property interest, however the small jurisdiction public generally exception applies,
